states.name: Idle
states.description: No active BDX session. Node is ready to initiate or respond to a transfer request.

states.name: SenderInitiatorWaitingAccept
states.description: Initiator has sent SendInit and is waiting for SendAccept or StatusReport from responder.

states.name: ReceiverInitiatorWaitingAccept
states.description: Initiator has sent ReceiveInit and is waiting for ReceiveAccept or StatusReport from responder.

states.name: SenderSyncSenderDrive
states.description: Sender is actively transferring data in synchronous mode with sender as driver. Sender sends Block/BlockEOF and waits for BlockAck/BlockAckEOF before proceeding.

states.name: SenderSyncReceiverDrive
states.description: Sender is in synchronous mode with receiver as driver. Sender waits for BlockQuery/BlockQueryWithSkip and responds with Block/BlockEOF.

states.name: ReceiverSyncSenderDrive
states.description: Receiver is in synchronous mode with sender as driver. Receiver receives Block/BlockEOF and sends BlockAck/BlockAckEOF.

states.name: ReceiverSyncReceiverDrive
states.description: Receiver is actively receiving data in synchronous mode as driver. Receiver sends BlockQuery/BlockQueryWithSkip and receives Block/BlockEOF.

states.name: SenderAsync
states.description: Sender is in asynchronous mode. Sends Block/BlockEOF without waiting for BlockAck. NOTE: Provisional mode, SHALL NOT be chosen by responder.

states.name: ReceiverAsync
states.description: Receiver is in asynchronous mode. Receives Block/BlockEOF without sending BlockAck, sends BlockAckEOF at end. NOTE: Provisional mode.

states.name: TransferComplete
states.description: BDX transfer completed successfully. BlockAckEOF has been sent/received confirming all data transferred.

states.name: TransferFailed
states.description: BDX transfer failed due to error. StatusReport sent/received or protocol violation detected.

actions: generate_send_init(version, transfer_modes, max_block_size, start_offset, length, file_designator, metadata)
generate_receive_init(version, transfer_modes, max_block_size, start_offset, length, file_designator, metadata)

transitions.note: Validation step, actual state transition follows based on results
Possible error codes: FILE_DESIGNATOR_UNKNOWN, LENGTH_TOO_LARGE, LENGTH_TOO_SHORT, LENGTH_REQUIRED, START_OFFSET_NOT_SUPPORTED, VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED, RESPONDER_BUSY, TRANSFER_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED

transitions.guard: validate_message_context() && accepted_mode == SENDER_DRIVE && accepted_version_compatible && accepted_block_size <= proposed_block_size
validate_message_context() && accepted_mode == RECEIVER_DRIVE && accepted_version_compatible && accepted_block_size <= proposed_block_size
validate_message_context() && accepted_mode == SENDER_DRIVE && accepted_version_compatible && accepted_block_size <= proposed_block_size
validate_message_context() && accepted_mode == RECEIVER_DRIVE && accepted_version_compatible && accepted_block_size <= proposed_block_size

transitions.note: Explicit BlockAck is optional in receiver-drive mode
After skip, if data remains, send Block
After skip, if at/past EOF, send empty BlockEOF per spec requirement
Waits for BlockAckEOF in next transition
Receiver may optionally send BlockAck before next query
Optional explicit ack, e.g., before going to sleep
PROVISIONAL MODE - SHALL NOT be chosen by responder
StatusReport can occur at any time to abort transfer

functions.name: validate_version
functions.behavior: Checks if the proposed protocol version is supported by this node. Returns true if the version is less than or equal to the maximum supported version.

functions.name: choose_version
functions.behavior: Selects the newest version that is supported by the responder and not newer than the proposed version. If no common version exists, returns 0 to signal version negotiation failure.

functions.name: validate_transfer_mode
functions.behavior: Checks if at least one of the proposed transfer modes (SENDER_DRIVE, RECEIVER_DRIVE, ASYNC) is supported by this node. Returns false if no modes are set or none are supported.

functions.name: choose_transfer_mode
functions.behavior: Selects exactly one transfer mode from the intersection of proposed and supported modes. Prefers SENDER_DRIVE over RECEIVER_DRIVE for request/response semantics. SHALL NOT choose ASYNC mode (provisional). Returns chosen mode or 0 if no common mode.

functions.name: validate_block_size
functions.behavior: Checks if the proposed maximum block size is acceptable. Returns true if within supported range.

functions.name: validate_start_offset
functions.behavior: Checks if non-zero start offset is supported and if the specific offset value is valid for the requested file. Returns true if offset is 0 or if non-zero offsets are supported and valid.

functions.name: validate_length
functions.behavior: Validates the proposed transfer length. Checks if length is too large, too short, or if definite length is required but not provided. Returns true if length is acceptable.

functions.name: validate_file_designator
functions.behavior: Checks if the file designator is known and supported by this node. Returns true if the designator is valid and the file/data can be accessed.

functions.name: check_responder_busy
functions.behavior: Determines if the node is too busy to accept a new BDX transfer at this moment. Returns true if busy, false if available.

functions.name: validate_message_security
functions.behavior: Verifies that the message is being received over a PASE or CASE encrypted session with proper authentication. Returns false if security requirements not met.

functions.name: validate_message_context
functions.behavior: Checks if the received message is contextually appropriate for the current state of the transfer. Returns false for UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE scenarios.

functions.name: validate_block_counter_order
functions.behavior: Validates that the block counter is exactly the expected sequential value. For Block/BlockEOF, expects exactly the next counter. For BlockAck/BlockAckEOF, expects the same counter as the last sent block. For BlockQuery, expects exactly next_counter = (last_counter + 1) mod 2^32. Returns false if out of order.

functions.name: increment_block_counter
functions.behavior: Increments the block counter using modulo 2^32 arithmetic. If current_counter is 0xFFFFFFFF, returns 0x00000000.

functions.name: validate_block_length
functions.behavior: For Block messages: validates 0 < data_length <= max_block_size. For BlockEOF messages: validates 0 <= data_length <= max_block_size. Returns true if valid.

functions.name: validate_transfer_length
functions.behavior: If a definite length was pre-negotiated, verifies that the total bytes received matches the expected length exactly. If indefinite length, returns true. Used when receiving BlockEOF.

functions.name: generate_send_init
functions.behavior: Constructs a SendInit message with the specified parameters. Sets PTC and RC fields appropriately based on which optional fields are present and their sizes.

functions.name: generate_receive_init
functions.behavior: Constructs a ReceiveInit message with the specified parameters. Sets PTC and RC fields appropriately based on which optional fields are present and their sizes.

functions.name: generate_send_accept
functions.behavior: Constructs a SendAccept message accepting the proposed SendInit. Sets TC field with exactly one transfer mode selected and the negotiated version. Max block size must be <= proposed max block size.

functions.name: generate_receive_accept
functions.behavior: Constructs a ReceiveAccept message accepting the proposed ReceiveInit. Sets TC and RC fields appropriately. Includes definite length if known. Max block size must be <= proposed max block size.

functions.name: generate_status_report
functions.behavior: Constructs a StatusReport message with the specified error codes. For BDX errors, protocol_id is PROTOCOL_ID_BDX and protocol_code is from Table 109.

functions.name: generate_block
functions.behavior: Constructs a Block message containing the block counter and data. Data length must be in range (0, max_block_size].

functions.name: generate_block_eof
functions.behavior: Constructs a BlockEOF message containing the final block counter and remaining data (may be empty). Data length must be in range [0, max_block_size].

functions.name: generate_block_ack
functions.behavior: Constructs a BlockAck message acknowledging receipt of a Block. Block counter must match the counter from the acknowledged Block.

functions.name: generate_block_ack_eof
functions.behavior: Constructs a BlockAckEOF message acknowledging receipt of BlockEOF and signaling successful transfer completion. Block counter must match the counter from the acknowledged BlockEOF.

functions.name: generate_block_query
functions.behavior: Constructs a BlockQuery message requesting the next block with the specified counter. Implies acknowledgement of previous block if no explicit BlockAck was sent.

functions.name: generate_block_query_with_skip
functions.behavior: Constructs a BlockQueryWithSkip message requesting the sender to skip forward by bytes_to_skip before sending the next block. Enables seeking within the transfer without terminating the session.

functions.name: extract_proposed_params
functions.behavior: Extracts all proposed parameters from a SendInit or ReceiveInit message including version, transfer modes, block size, offsets, lengths, file designator, and metadata.

functions.name: extract_version
functions.behavior: Extracts the negotiated protocol version from the TC field of a SendAccept or ReceiveAccept message.

functions.name: extract_block_size
functions.behavior: Extracts the negotiated max block size from a SendAccept or ReceiveAccept message.

functions.name: extract_length
functions.behavior: Extracts the definite transfer length from a ReceiveAccept message if RC[DEFLEN] is set, otherwise returns null for indefinite length.

functions.name: extract_metadata
functions.behavior: Extracts optional TLV metadata from the message if present.

functions.name: extract_data
functions.behavior: Extracts the data payload from a Block or BlockEOF message. Data field length is the remainder of the message after the block counter.

functions.name: extract_block_data
functions.behavior: Retrieves the data to be sent for the specified block counter. Returns up to max_size bytes from the source file/stream based on current transfer position.

functions.name: extract_remaining_data
functions.behavior: Retrieves the final remaining data for BlockEOF. May return empty bytes if no data remains (e.g., after skip operation reaches EOF).

functions.name: extract_skip_amount
functions.behavior: Extracts the BytesToSkip field from a BlockQueryWithSkip message indicating how many bytes to skip forward in the stream.

functions.name: extract_protocol_code
functions.behavior: Extracts the protocol-specific error code from a StatusReport message for the BDX protocol.

functions.name: skip_bytes
functions.behavior: Advances the cursor within the underlying data stream by the specified number of bytes. If skip reaches or exceeds EOF, cursor is positioned at EOF and next operation returns empty BlockEOF.

functions.name: more_data_remaining_after_skip
functions.behavior: Determines if data will remain after skipping the specified number of bytes. Returns false if skip would reach or exceed EOF. Used to decide whether to send Block or empty BlockEOF after BlockQueryWithSkip.

functions.name: check_if_cursor_at_end
functions.behavior: Returns true if the current cursor position in the data stream is at or beyond the end of the transferable data.

functions.name: store_data
functions.behavior: Stores received block data to the destination (file, memory buffer, etc.). May trigger I/O operations.

functions.name: verify_transfer_complete
functions.behavior: Performs final verification that the transfer is complete and valid. If definite length was specified, asserts that bytes_received matches expected_length exactly.

functions.name: determine_error_code
functions.behavior: Maps validation failure reasons to appropriate BDX protocol error codes from Table 109. Returns codes like FILE_DESIGNATOR_UNKNOWN, LENGTH_TOO_LARGE, VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED, etc.

functions.name: start_response_timer
functions.behavior: Starts an application-determined timeout timer for waiting for response messages. If timer expires before response received, transfer SHOULD be aborted.

functions.name: cancel_response_timer
functions.behavior: Cancels the response timeout timer when expected response is received.

functions.name: send_message
functions.behavior: Sends a BDX protocol message over the active encrypted session using the reliable transport. Message is protected by PASE/CASE session security.

functions.name: abort_transfer
functions.behavior: Immediately terminates the BDX transfer session, releases resources, and invalidates the exchange. Called on errors or protocol violations.

functions.name: log_error
functions.behavior: Records error information for debugging and diagnostics.

functions.name: log_explicit_ack
functions.behavior: Records that an explicit (optional) BlockAck was received in receiver-drive mode.

definitions.definition: The node that has bulk data to send to another node.
The node that receives bulk data from a Sender.
The node that initiates a bulk data transfer. Can be either Sender (upload via SendInit) or Receiver (download via ReceiveInit).
The node that responds to the initiator by accepting or rejecting the proposed transfer and choosing final parameters. Can be either Sender or Receiver depending on the init message received.
Transfer mode where one party (Driver) controls the rate and each message SHALL be acknowledged before the next is sent. Enables intermittently connected devices to complete transfers.

Transfer mode with no driver where successive messages are freely sent without waiting for BlockAck. Flow control provided by underlying transport. NOTE: Provisional mode, SHALL NOT be chosen by responder.
In synchronous mode, the node (Sender or Receiver) that controls transfer rate by sending Block or BlockQuery messages. Transfer consists of request/response pairs initiated by driver.
In synchronous mode, the node that follows the driver. Never sleepy. Either receives BlockQuery and sends data, or receives Block and sends BlockAck.
A series of BDX messages within a single Exchange, beginning with SendInit/ReceiveInit and ending with BlockAckEOF. Only one BDX session per Exchange.
Protocol identifier for BDX protocol. VendorID=0x0000, ProtocolID=specific value for BDX.
32-bit unsigned integer identifying blocks in sequential order. Uses modulo 2^32 arithmetic (wraps from 0xFFFFFFFF to 0x00000000). Starts at 0 for each transfer.
Variable-length identifier chosen by Initiator to identify the payload to be transferred. May be pre-negotiated or interpreted with metadata.
Optional TLV-encoded application-specific information about the transfer, allowing decisions without additional round-trips.
Maximum data payload size (in bytes) for each Block/BlockEOF message, exclusive of headers like block counter. Negotiated between initiator and responder.
Pre-negotiated total transfer size. For SendInit, number of bytes sender commits to send. For ReceiveInit, max bytes receiver wishes to receive. Presence indicated by RC[DEFLEN] bit.
Transfer with unknown or unlimited size (length=0 or field absent). Final size determined when BlockEOF is sent.
Offset in bytes from start of file where transfer begins. Enables segmented large file transfers across multiple sessions. Presence indicated by RC[STARTOFS] bit.
When RC[WIDERANGE]=1, offset and length fields use 64-bit values. When 0, fields use 32-bit values.

security_properties.description: All BDX messages SHALL be transmitted only over PASE or CASE encrypted sessions. Data in transit is protected from eavesdropping.
security_properties.enforcement: Protocol requirement: BDX SHALL only execute over PASE/CASE. Enforced by only allowing BDX messages within secured sessions.

security_properties.description: All BDX messages are authenticated through the underlying PASE or CASE session. Peer identity is cryptographically verified.
security_properties.enforcement: Provided by PASE/CASE session establishment. BDX inherits authentication from session layer.

security_properties.description: All BDX messages are protected against tampering through PASE/CASE encryption and MACs. Any modification detected results in message rejection.
security_properties.enforcement: Provided by PASE/CASE message security. BDX inherits integrity protection from session layer.

security_properties.description: BDX SHALL be used only over reliable transports (MRP, TCP) to ensure delivery and ordering of messages.
security_properties.enforcement: Protocol requirement: BDX SHALL use reliable transport. Not allowed over UDP without MRP.

security_properties.description: Transport-level reliability provides at-most-once delivery semantics. Duplicate messages are prevented by underlying message layer.
security_properties.enforcement: Provided by Matter messaging layer and reliable transport protocols.

security_properties.description: Block counters must be strictly sequential. Out-of-order blocks are rejected with BAD_BLOCK_COUNTER error.
security_properties.enforcement: FSM validates block counter order on every Block/BlockAck/BlockQuery/BlockEOF message. Any deviation triggers immediate abort.

security_properties.description: Only one BDX session can be active within a single Exchange. Multiple concurrent BDX sessions require separate Exchanges.
security_properties.enforcement: Protocol requirement: single session per Exchange. Attempting to start new session in active exchange results in rejection.

security_properties.description: If definite length is pre-negotiated, receiver must verify exact match between expected and received data. Mismatch results in LENGTH_MISMATCH error.
security_properties.enforcement: FSM checks bytes_received == expected_length when receiving BlockEOF. Transfer fails if mismatch detected.

security_properties.description: Responder can reject transfers with RESPONDER_BUSY if unable to handle new transfer. Provides DoS protection.
security_properties.enforcement: Responder checks availability before accepting init. Returns RESPONDER_BUSY status if cannot proceed.

security_properties.description: Block data length must not exceed negotiated max block size. Enforces agreed-upon resource limits.
security_properties.enforcement: FSM validates received block data length <= max_block_size for all Block/BlockEOF messages.

security_properties.description: Any protocol violation or error condition results in immediate transfer abort and exchange invalidation. Fail-fast behavior.
security_properties.enforcement: FSM transitions to TransferFailed state and aborts on any validation failure, unexpected message, or error condition.

security_properties.description: Prevents version downgrade attacks. Responder selects highest common version not exceeding initiator's proposal. Rejects if no common version.
security_properties.enforcement: FSM validates version compatibility. Returns VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED if no common version exists.

security_properties.description: Both parties must agree on exactly one transfer mode. Prevents protocol confusion.
security_properties.enforcement: Responder selects exactly one mode from initiator's proposals. Initiator must accept or abort. Mode mismatch triggers TRANSFER_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED.

cryptographic_operations.description: All BDX messages are encrypted and authenticated via the underlying PASE or CASE session. BDX does not perform cryptographic operations directly.
cryptographic_operations.purpose: Provides confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity for all BDX communications

security_assumptions.description: BDX relies entirely on the underlying PASE or CASE session for all cryptographic security (encryption, authentication, integrity). The session must be properly established and maintained.
security_assumptions.justification: BDX protocol specification mandates execution only over PASE/CASE sessions.

security_assumptions.description: BDX assumes the underlying transport (MRP, TCP) provides reliable, ordered delivery. Protocol correctness depends on messages not being lost, duplicated, or reordered by transport.
security_assumptions.justification: Specification requires BDX SHALL use reliable transport. Application-level flow control depends on this guarantee.

security_assumptions.description: BDX assumes only one session is active within an Exchange. Concurrent sessions would violate protocol state machine.
security_assumptions.justification: Specification states only one BDX session can be in progress at any time during an Exchange.

security_assumptions.description: Protocol assumes block counters are strictly sequential and uses this for implicit acknowledgements and ordering. Breaking this assumption leads to BAD_BLOCK_COUNTER errors.
security_assumptions.justification: Block ordering rules mandate sequential counters with modulo 2^32 arithmetic.

security_assumptions.description: Driver in synchronous mode relies on application-determined timeouts to detect failures when expected responses don't arrive.
security_assumptions.justification: Specification states driver SHOULD abort if response not received within application-determined time.

security_assumptions.description: Follower in synchronous mode is assumed to be non-sleepy and always able to respond when driver sends messages.
security_assumptions.justification: Protocol defines followers as devices that can never be sleepy.

security_assumptions.description: Nodes are expected to properly manage resources and indicate RESPONDER_BUSY when unable to accept transfers, preventing resource exhaustion.
security_assumptions.justification: Specification provides RESPONDER_BUSY error code for this purpose.

security_assumptions.description: When definite length is specified, it is assumed to be accurate. Sender must send exactly that amount; receiver validates this.
security_assumptions.justification: Protocol requires LENGTH_MISMATCH error if pre-negotiated size not fulfilled.

validation_checklist.all_conditions_notes: All conditional flows from spec modeled: version negotiation, mode selection, validation checks, block ordering rules, length validation, error conditions, optional vs required fields, synchronous vs asynchronous modes (provisional), driver vs follower roles, sender vs receiver roles.
validation_checklist.cryptographic_notes: BDX does not perform direct cryptographic operations. All security inherited from PASE/CASE session encryption and authentication, which is detailed in security_assumptions.
validation_checklist.error_states_notes: TransferFailed state captures all error scenarios. All status codes from Table 109 modeled: LENGTH_TOO_LARGE, LENGTH_TOO_SHORT, LENGTH_MISMATCH, LENGTH_REQUIRED, BAD_MESSAGE_CONTENTS, BAD_BLOCK_COUNTER, UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, RESPONDER_BUSY, TRANSFER_FAILED_UNKNOWN_ERROR, TRANSFER_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED, FILE_DESIGNATOR_UNKNOWN, START_OFFSET_NOT_SUPPORTED, VERSION_NOT_SUPPORTED, UNKNOWN.
validation_checklist.timers_notes: Response timeouts modeled via start_response_timer/cancel_response_timer functions and response_timeout trigger. Application-determined timeout for driver waiting for responses. Transport-level timeouts inherited from reliable transport.
validation_checklist.functions_notes: All 43 functions defined with parameters, return types, and behavior. Covers validation, generation, extraction, error handling, timer management, and transport operations. Includes more_data_remaining_after_skip for proper BlockQueryWithSkip response handling. All functions actually used in transitions are listed in all_functions_used.
validation_checklist.guards_notes: Guards cover all decision points: validation success/failure, mode selection, counter ordering, length checks, timeout conditions, busy states, security validation. Complementary guards ensure all cases handled, including separate guards for BlockQueryWithSkip depending on whether skip reaches EOF.
validation_checklist.actions_notes: All actions are atomic assignments, function calls, or event generation. No conditionals or loops in actions. Complex logic encapsulated in functions.
validation_checklist.security_properties_notes: 13 security properties identified covering confidentiality, authentication, integrity, reliability, ordering, session isolation, validation requirements, resource control, and error handling.
validation_checklist.spec_coverage_notes: FSM covers all message types (8 opcodes: SendInit, SendAccept, ReceiveInit, ReceiveAccept, BlockQuery, BlockQueryWithSkip, Block, BlockEOF, BlockAck, BlockAckEOF), all status codes, all roles (sender/receiver/initiator/responder/driver/follower), all modes (sender-drive, receiver-drive, async-provisional), all validation rules, all error conditions, all sequence diagrams from section 11.22.7. BlockQueryWithSkip properly modeled with sender immediately responding with Block or empty BlockEOF based on whether skip reaches EOF, per section 11.22.6.3 requirements.
validation_checklist.proverif_tamarin_notes: FSM structure compatible: states with explicit attributes, transitions with guards and atomic actions, functions with clear signatures, security properties explicitly enumerated. Block counter modulo arithmetic may need special encoding. PASE/CASE session security would be modeled as symbolic encryption in ProVerif.
